etymology.description: 白(白) (bái, "spring mouth") + 水(水) (shuǐ, "water") = water flowing from the source of a spring -> spring
etymology.interpretation: The character combines 白(白) (bái, "spring mouth") with 水(水) (shuǐ, "water") to depict the concept of water emerging from the origin of a spring. This imagery conveys the idea of a fresh water source coming out from an opening, which is metaphorically the 'mouth' of the spring.

examples.chinese: 山脚下有一个清澈的泉(山腳下有一個清澈的泉)。
examples.pinyin: Shānjiǎo xià yǒu yīgè qīngchè de quán.
examples.english: There is a clear spring at the foot of the mountain.

examples.chinese: 村民用泉水灌溉农田(村民用泉水灌溉農田)。
examples.english: The villagers use spring water to irrigate the fields.

examples.chinese: 这个地方以温泉而闻名(這個地方以溫泉而聞名)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège dìfāng yǐ wēnquán ér wénmíng.
examples.english: This place is famous for its hot springs.
